name: RC-Immix — Region Coloring for Reference-Counting Allocators
summary: Shahriyar, Blackburn, Yang, McKinley (OOPSLA 2013) marry reference counting with Immix's mark-region heap (lines + blocks) for high-throughput RC. Per-line live-object counts (a coarse 'region color') let the collector reclaim whole lines when the line count hits zero, avoiding free-list fragmentation. Region granularity is independent of language-level region semantics — purely a layout/locality optimization. LXR extends this lineage with low-latency region-coloured RC.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Not a region *language* feature but a *runtime* layout technique. Shahriyar, Blackburn, Yang, McKinley (OOPSLA 2013) marry reference counting with **Immix's mark-region heap** (lines + blocks) for high-throughput RC. **Per-line live-object counts** (a coarse "region color") let the collector reclaim whole lines when the line count hits zero, avoiding free-list fragmentation.

Region granularity is independent of language-level region semantics — purely a **layout/locality optimization**. The lesson is that the same word "region" means two different things across this chapter and the chapter on tracing GC architectures: programmer-visible regions vs. allocator-internal regions.

sections.title: Line-granular reclamation and copying
sections.content:
Immix divides the heap into 32 KiB **blocks**, each containing 256-byte **lines**. RC-Immix tracks a per-line live-object count: incremented when an object is allocated into the line, decremented when the object's RC reaches zero. When a line's count hits zero the entire line is reclaimable as one unit — no free-list traversal, no per-object work.

Proactive + reactive copying integrated with RC eliminates fragmentation; backup tracing handles cycles. The result is RC throughput competitive with tracing GC on heap-intensive workloads, with the per-line region as the load-bearing data structure.

sections.title: LXR — low-latency region-coloured RC
sections.content:
**LXR** extends this lineage with low-latency region-coloured RC, targeting modern multicore latency budgets. The same line-as-region primitive is retained, but the collector's coordination protocol is reworked to keep mutator pause times short under high allocation rates.

LXR is documented in detail in the tracing-GC chapter; this entry only marks the link from the original RC-Immix work to its successor. For a language runtime designer choosing a high-throughput RC collector in 2026, LXR is the modern reference and RC-Immix is the foundational paper.
